denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 04/05/2026
Cigna Open Access Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W103421008          Group #: GRP-1432
Authorization / Reference #: PA-2026-163205
Claim #: CLM-2026-210897
Date of Service (requested): 03/16/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Harborview Clinical Partners

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's prior authorization request on 03/18/2026 for MRI lumbar spine without contrast related to Lumbar disc herniation, suspected (M51.26). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: MRI lumbar spine without contrast
Diagnosis (as submitted): Lumbar disc herniation, suspected (M51.26)
Denial code: CO-197 — Precertification/authorization absent — service requires prior approval before the date of service.
Clinical policy applied: Coverage Policy 132 under Cigna Medical Coverage Policy (CPG) and Milliman Care Guidelines (MCG)

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
This determination is based on a plan exclusion. State coverage mandates do not alter the terms of this plan for this determination.
Our review applied the criteria in Coverage Policy 132 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. MRI lumbar spine without contrast is non-preferred; a formulary alternative must be tried for Lumbar disc herniation, suspected (M51.26). A lower-tier agent is available without demonstrated failure of the preferred option.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with formulary tier dispute. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file and the clinical criteria we used in this determination. Submit your request to the address on this notice or through your plan's member portal.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a Cigna medical director regarding this determination.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W103421008) and reference PA-2026-163205. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination.

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Cigna Health Care Management
Medical Review Unit
clinical_context: The 42-year-old member has Lumbar disc herniation, suspected (M51.26). She has been treated under commercial coverage with MRI lumbar spine without contrast recommended as the next appropriate step. Radicular pain 6 weeks; failed PT; straight-leg raise positive. The prior-authorization packet included dated clinic notes, medication trials, and objective findings supporting medical necessity. The denial rationale does not reference those submissions. Treating clinicians document ongoing functional impairment and recommend proceeding without further delay when benefit-plan criteria have been met based on the record on file.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic Cigna correspondence with MCG Care Guidelines and procedural tone.
